name: Y2K Bubblegum Carousel
aesthetic: Y2K bubblegum nostalgia — lilac/pink/blue/yellow pastels, oval + polaroid photo frames tilted at playful angles, white daisies, puffy clouds, hand-drawn squiggles and scribbles, white paintbrush stroke text backgrounds, brush script + bold Y2K display type. Feels like a 2003 MySpace banner meets a sticker pack.

fonts.script_brush: Caveat Brush / Permanent Marker (handwritten slide titles)
fonts.display_bold: Bungee / Rubik Mono One (big Y2K display words with stroke shadow)

slides.subject: Tilted oval photo frame centered on bubblegum block; big bold Y2K title with stroke shadow arcs above; daisies and clouds scattered; handle pill below.
Tilted polaroid frame left with dish photo; right column has white brush-stroke headline (script) and uppercase sub. Sky-blue background.
slides.decor: Daisies tucked behind polaroid; scribble accents; a single lemon or cloud

slides.subject: Lilac background with a big circle photo (bubblegum ring) centered; brush-stroke white banner behind handwritten headline at top; painted swash at bottom with handle.
slides.decor: Daisies, 2 clouds, squiggles around the circle

slides.subject: Two stacked tilted photo frames (one rectangular, one polaroid) on pink background; bold Y2K display headline top-right with stroke shadow; neighbourhood pill bottom-right.
Bubblegum/lilac split background; BiteMap logo card centered; 'catch u on' script + 'BiteMap.' + app badges; daisies and clouds framing the edges.
slides.copy: Fixed: 'catch u on' / BiteMap logo / tagline / app badges

copy_rules: Bold display words (slides 1, 4) are ONE or TWO words, UPPERCASE, short — they carry the Y2K stroke-shadow styling
Script words (slides 2, 3) are handwritten-feel phrases — 1-3 words max
Restaurant name goes only in the @handle pill — never in the big display word
Never use generic hype ('amazing', 'must-try'). Name the literal thing.
Tone is playful and nostalgic, not corporate
asset_rules: Photos must be bright, colorful, high-contrast — dim interior shots fight the pastel palette
Minimum 1080px wide; real photos only (no AI)
Ovals and circles crop tight to the subject — pick images with the dish centered or a clear portrait
use_when: Gen-Z leaning restaurants (bubble tea, dessert cafes, smash burgers, Korean corn dogs, Thai boba)
Playful product drops (new menu, collab, merch)
Lighter / sweeter cuisines where pastel palette is on-brand
